title: spartina maritima subsp. glabra
intro: Spartina maritima subsp. glabra, also known as Smooth Cordgrass, is a subspecies of the Spartina maritima plant, a type of perennial grass that is native to the Atlantic coast of Europe. Traditionally, Smooth Cordgrass has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been reported to help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat wounds. The plant is also known for its health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of Smooth Cordgrass are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and reduce the risk of various diseases.
